name: Vegetable Lasagna Roll-Ups
recipeIngredient: 8 ounces Fresh spinach, washed and chopped
1 can (15 oz) white kidney beans, drained and rinsed
3 teaspoons Extra-light olive oil with a dash of sesame oil
1 large Red bell pepper, seeded and finely diced
1 can (16 oz) whole tomatoes with liquid
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine in a large mixing bowl the three cheeses, spinach, half the beans, half the oregano, and half the basil. Set aside.
2. Cook the noodles al dente. Cool them under cold water when they're cooked to your liking.
3. Preheat the oven to 350°F. Pour 1 teaspoon of oil into a large skillet over medium-high heat and fry onions and garlic for 5 minutes, stirring often.
4. Transfer half of the spinach and cheese filling to one pan; set the other half aside.
5. Add 1 teaspoon of oil to the same pan and cook eggplant over medium heat for 8 minutes, stirring often. Do not let the bottom of the pan scorch. Spoon the cooked eggplant into the filling mixture and stir well.
6. Wipe the pan clean.
7. Add the remaining oil to the pan and cook red bell pepper over medium heat for 3 minutes. Spoon the cooked pepper into the filling mixture.
8. Wipe the pan clean.
9. Add tomato paste to the same pan and cook over medium heat until it turns brown, about 5 minutes. Ensure it doesn't burn but just browns.
10. Stir in red pepper flakes and wine, bring to a boil, lower the heat to a simmer, and reduce the liquid by about one fourth (15 minutes).
11. Add canned tomatoes and their liquid, stirring until the tomatoes break into pieces.
12. Add remaining beans, oregano, basil, and reserved cooked onions and garlic; cook for 5 minutes at a very low simmer. Stir in lemon juice, salt, pepper, and mix well.
13. Lay lasagna noodles flat on a cutting board. Form half of the filling into rough balls, lay one end on a noodle, and roll up end to end. Repeat with remaining noodles.
14. Pour sauce into a 9" X 13" baking pan. Place lasagna rolls seam side down, spooning some sauce over them. Cover with aluminum foil and bake for 40 minutes.
15. Remove foil, sprinkle with Parmesan cheese, and bake for an additional 5 minutes.
